eli3: LLM serving at scale handles billions of tokens daily for applications like ChatGPT, Claude, and Copilot. Unlike traditional ML, LLMs generate text token-by-token, requiring iterative inference and massive GPU memory.

Unique challenges: autoregressive generation (one token at a time), KV-cache memory explosion (storing attention keys/values for all previous tokens), and variable-length outputs (can't batch efficiently like classification).
Optimization techniques: KV-cache management, continuous batching, model parallelism across GPUs, quantization to reduce memory, and speculative decoding to speed up generation.

whenWhy.title: Chatbots and assistants
whenWhy.body: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini: conversational AI with millions of users. Sub-second first token latency, streaming subsequent tokens. Handle concurrent conversations at massive scale. Cost dominated by inference, not training.

whenWhy.title: Code generation
whenWhy.body: GitHub Copilot, Codeium: autocomplete and generate code. Low latency critical (<100ms for suggestions). Context window: entire file + related files. High throughput for IDE integration.

whenWhy.title: Enterprise search and RAG
whenWhy.body: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: retrieve documents, generate answers. Long context windows (100k+ tokens). Batching challenging due to variable document lengths. Quality vs latency tradeoffs.

whenWhy.title: Content generation at scale
whenWhy.body: Marketing copy, summarization, translation. Batch processing for bulk generation. Cost optimization crucial (millions of API calls). Quality consistency across batches.

deepDiveIntro: LLM inference has two phases: (1) Prefill: process input prompt in parallel, compute KV-cache for all prompt tokens. (2) Decode: generate output tokens autoregressively (one at a time), using cached KVs. Prefill is compute-bound (can parallelize). Decode is memory-bound (sequential, waiting on memory bandwidth).

Memory challenges: Model parameters (70B model = 140GB FP16). KV-cache per request: 2 × layers × heads × head_dim × seq_len × bytes. For 4096 length, 80 layers: ~5GB per request! Limits concurrent requests. Solutions: paging (vLLM), quantization (4-bit), sparse attention.

deepDive.heading: KV-Cache Management
deepDive.body: Attention requires keys and values for all previous tokens. Cache avoids recomputation. Memory grows with sequence length and batch size. vLLM's PagedAttention: allocate KV-cache in non-contiguous blocks like OS virtual memory. Reduces fragmentation, enables efficient memory sharing (parallel sampling). 2-4x throughput improvement.
deepDive.formula: KV_cache_size = 2 × num_layers × num_heads × head_dim × seq_len × sizeof(dtype)

deepDive.heading: Continuous Batching
deepDive.body: Static batching: wait for all sequences to complete (slow ones block batch). Continuous (inflight) batching: add new requests and complete finished ones dynamically. Orchestration layer manages batch composition. 10-20x throughput vs static for variable-length generation. Key innovation in vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, TGI.

deepDive.heading: Model Parallelism for LLMs
deepDive.body: Single GPU can't hold large models (GPT-4 estimated 1.8T parameters = 3.6TB). Tensor parallelism: split layers across GPUs within node (NVLink). Pipeline parallelism: split layers across nodes. Data parallelism: replicate for throughput. ZeRO partitioning: shard optimizer states. 3D parallelism combines all.

deepDive.heading: Quantization
deepDive.body: Reduce memory and increase speed. INT8: 2x smaller, minimal accuracy loss. INT4/GPTQ: 4x smaller, some degradation. AWQ: activation-aware quantization, better than GPTQ. SmoothQuant: balances difficulty of quantizing activations vs weights. FlashAttention-2: IO-aware algorithm reduces HBM reads. 2-4x speedup.

deepDive.heading: Speculative Decoding
deepDive.body: Small draft model generates K candidate tokens quickly. Large target model verifies all K in parallel (single forward pass). Accept tokens up to first rejection. Speedup 2-3x if draft model quality reasonable. Draft can be smaller version of same model or specialized n-gram model. Medusa: multiple draft heads, tree attention for verification.

takeaways: KV-cache dominates memory: vLLM's PagedAttention optimizes.
Continuous batching: 10-20x throughput for variable lengths.
Quantization: INT8/INT4 reduce memory 2-4x with minimal accuracy loss.
Speculative decoding: draft model + parallel verification = 2-3x speedup.

edgeCases.q: How do you handle context length limits?
edgeCases.a: Models have max context (4k, 32k, 100k+ tokens). Solutions: (1) Truncation: drop oldest tokens (lose early context). (2) Summarization: compress long context recursively. (3) RAG: retrieve relevant chunks instead of full context. (4) Long-context models: ALiBi, RoPE scaling, but slower. (5) Sliding window attention: tradeoff between local and global context.

edgeCases.q: What's the cost breakdown for LLM serving?
edgeCases.a: Dominant costs: (1) GPU capital + operational (electricity, cooling) — 70%+. (2) Engineering: optimization, infrastructure — 20%. (3) Networking: especially for distributed serving — 10%. Per-token cost decreases with batch size but increases latency. Optimization priority: maximize batch size while meeting latency SLO. Spot/preemptible instances for batch workloads.

edgeCases.q: How do you ensure consistent output quality at scale?
edgeCases.a: Challenges: temperature sampling introduces randomness, different outputs each time. Solutions: (1) Seed control: fix random seed for reproducibility. (2) Sampling parameters: top_p, top_k, temperature tuning. (3) A/B testing: measure quality metrics (BLEU, human eval) continuously. (4) Canary deployments: test new model versions on small traffic. (5) Output caching: store common queries. (6) Guardrails: post-processing filters for safety/policy.

sources.label: Kwon et al. — Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
Pope et al. — Efficiently Scaling Transformer Inference
